name: Chestnut Roast
recipeIngredient: Fresh breadcrumbs (Optional) – should be whole wheat
recipeInstructions:
------------------------
FOR COATING
-------------
Dried breadcrumbs
Melt the butter (or substitute) in a saucepan over moderate heat. Add the onion and celery, sautéing until soft but not brown, about 10 minutes.
Transfer to a large bowl. Drain the chestnuts and add them to the bowl. Mix well with the melted butter mixture.
Add chopped parsley, lemon juice, and crushed garlic. If needed, sprinkle in some whole-wheat breadcrumbs to help bind the mixture; season with salt and pepper.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Pour a little oil into a roasting pan and heat it briefly in the oven.
Form the chestnut mixture into a roll about 8 inches long, pressing it together carefully. Coat it well with dried breadcrumbs.
Place the roll in the hot roasting pan so that it is fully coated with hot oil. Bake for 45 minutes, spooning additional oil over the roll as needed during cooking.
Serve the Chestnut Roast cut into slices.
